name: brew
description: The de-facto macOS package manager (also works on Linux). Frequently the missing prerequisite for everything else.

installs.notes: Two-step review flow: download, show the script, then `/bin/bash /tmp/brew-install.sh`. The script itself invokes sudo to create /opt/homebrew (Apple Silicon) or /usr/local (Intel). After install, PATH needs `eval "$(/opt/homebrew/bin/brew shellenv)"` in the rc file — the installer prints this and users routinely skip it.
Same review flow. Installs to /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew; needs build tools (`build-essential` on Debian/Ubuntu) and the same shellenv rc line.

commonFailures: `brew: command not found` immediately after a successful install — the shellenv line was never added to the rc file, or the shell wasn't restarted. This is the single most common Homebrew issue.
On Apple Silicon, an old Intel-era /usr/local Homebrew coexisting with /opt/homebrew causes formula/arch mismatches.
Install fails early if Xcode Command Line Tools are missing; `xcode-select --install` is the prerequisite.
